extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r3 (priority 50), whose antecedent is (g6pd_deficiency AND peptic_ulcer AND seizure_history AND diabetes AND anticoagulated). rec_drug_b is set true only by r5 (priority 50), whose antecedent is g6pd_deficiency. Because r3's antecedent already requires g6pd_deficiency=true, whenever r3 fires, r5 necessarily also fires. With g6pd_deficiency, peptic_ulcer, seizure_history, diabetes, and anticoagulated all true, r3 sets rec_drug_a=true and r5 sets rec_drug_b=true. These outputs are written by distinct rules, so there is no priority/tie conflict that could suppress either, and the defaults only apply to outputs left unset. Thus both rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are jointly true, violating the mutual-exclusion property. The remaining inputs (bradycardia, polypharmacy, hypertension) are irrelevant and set false here.
